name: How long do I have to file a claim after a construction injury in Utah?
description: Utah: 4 years for lawsuits, report workers' comp ASAP (180 days max).

mainEntity.name: How long do I have to file a claim after a construction injury in Utah?

acceptedAnswer.text: Utah: 4 years for lawsuits, report workers' comp ASAP (180 days max).
acceptedAnswer.description: **In Utah, you generally have 4 years from the injury date to file a personal injury lawsuit, but workers' comp claims must be filed within 180 days to one year depending on knowledge of injury.** Shorter deadlines apply if government involved[3]. **Key factors:** 1. Workers' comp: Report immediately, file formally soon after. 2. Third-party suits: 4-year statute, but act fast for evidence. 3. Idaho nearby has 2 years for personal injury[2]. 4. Delays risk evidence loss as sites change[3]. Preserve photos, witness contacts early. Consult a lawyer promptly to meet all deadlines and build your case.
